body_txt: The first team is investigating a man found shot dead downtown. In the B-plot, the second team is examining a woman found strangled dead in her bathroom. Turns out they both had the same hairdresser, who is revealed to be the killer. Both teams were Working the Same Case. This is a useful technique to add a minor twist to help shows with two concurrent cases feel a little less formulaic/disjointed. Used at least once a season in CSI . Can also sometimes be used between two normally separate works for a Crossover . A variant is for the senior officer, fed up of the Cowboy Cop 's attitude, to tell him he won't be involved in the big murder case and will be investigating a break-in at a laundromat the night before. The Cowboy Cop will soon realise, possibly in a "Eureka!" Moment , that it's the same case. He will be careful not to let anyone else in on this, assuring his superiors (truthfully) that he's still following up that laundromat robbery. See also "Strangers on a Train"-Plot Murder , Minor Crime Reveals Major Plot , Hyperlink Story .
